title: Dev Env UI mode dropdown + buildSessionRequest + LIVE-verified placement front door

tests.summary: 10 passed — request shapes (orchestrated/bare/resume), placement (joe→expert→opus, channel defaults, null-when-absent)

suite.summary: 887 passed; 6 PRE-EXISTING failures (flaky-counted as 6-7); no new regressions; none of the failing files import dev-env UI

live.summary: LIVE front door returns {mode:joe, role:expert, model:opus, effort:high}; composed prompts written to ~/.garrison/dev-env-souls/{joe,james}.md. This live check CAUGHT a real bug (dynamic .mjs import fails in the Next runtime → role/model null) which was fixed by inlining the bias as pure TS, then re-verified.

review.summary: additive dialog control reusing existing .modal-label/.project-picker styling; pure buildSessionRequest; placement fix inlines bias (no runtime .mjs import)

codexReview.lastReport: UI diff APPROVED round 1; the placement inline-bias FIX could NOT be cross-model-reviewed — Codex workspace ran OUT OF CREDITS (codex exec returned 'Your workspace is out of credits. Add credits to continue.'). External blocker.

designAudit.reason: additive mode <select> reusing the adjacent Project picker's exact styling (.modal-label/.project-picker) — no new visual language. A live screenshot pass is deferred (needs the dev-env UI booted).

video.reason: functional evidence is the LIVE API integration check (correct joe/expert/opus placement + composed prompts written); a screen-recorded walkthrough needs the full live UI drive + tailscale and is not recorded in this autonomous context.

blockers.cause: external: Codex/ChatGPT workspace out of credits
blockers.observed: Your workspace is out of credits. Add credits to continue.
blockers.remediation: Add credits to the Codex (ChatGPT) workspace, then re-run the adversarial review/test gates.

codexReview.round1Findings: r1 seed-hardwiring -> r2 always-default -> r3 runner-doesn't-set-env -> r4 stale-env-on-running-devenv: all addressed (composition threaded route+caller+runner GARRISON_COMPOSITION_ID); r5 confirmed the residual already-running edge is not-applicable to the single-active-composition/single-shared-dev-env model (env-less placement falls back to the actual active 'default' composition)

codexPwTest.note: independent cross-model functional pass: Codex ran the modes-arc committed suite (14/14 files, 93/93 tests) + spot-checked specs against production code for non-vacuousness. Backend slices verified via independent committed-spec execution (no running UI to Playwright-drive); s3c UI also has the dev-env walkthrough.
